meta.notes: Level 3 topics weighted to appear ~2x more frequently than Level 2 or Level 1. Each topic is a specific sub-topic within a module, not the module itself. Full cycle = 33 days before any topic repeats.

schedule.topic: Residual Valuation: Calculating Site Value, GDV and Developer's Profit
schedule.focus: How to structure a residual appraisal from scratch — GDV build-up, construction costs, fees and the derivation of land value or developer's profit as the balancing figure.

schedule.topic: Types of Planning Application: Full, Outline, Reserved Matters and Prior Approval
schedule.focus: When to use each application type, what information is required, the role of indicative details in outline applications, and the reserved matters submission process.

schedule.topic: Terms of Engagement, Scope of Service and Complaints Procedures
schedule.focus: RICS requirements for written terms of engagement, defining scope to manage expectations, handling formal complaints and the role of the RICS Dispute Resolution Service.

schedule.module: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism
schedule.topic: RICS Rules of Conduct 2021: The Five Standards in Practice
schedule.focus: The five standards (Integrity, Competence, Service, Business, Promotion), how they apply to day-to-day P&D work, and examples of conduct that would breach them.
schedule.apc_competency: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism (Level 3)

schedule.topic: Development Finance Structures: Senior Debt, Mezzanine and Equity
schedule.focus: How development projects are funded determines whether they proceed at all. Understanding the capital stack — how senior debt, mezzanine finance and equity are layered — is essential for advising clients on viability, working with lenders, and passing your APC at Level 3.

schedule.topic: Reading Development Accounts: P&L, Balance Sheets and Basic Financial Ratios
schedule.focus: How to interpret a profit and loss account and balance sheet in a development context, and the key ratios (gearing, liquidity, return on capital) a surveyor should understand.

schedule.topic: Sensitivity Analysis: Testing Assumptions and Identifying Key Risk Drivers
schedule.focus: How to run single-variable and scenario sensitivity analyses on a development appraisal, which inputs to stress-test first (GDV, build cost, finance rate), and how results inform decision-making.

schedule.topic: Material Considerations in Planning Decisions: The Weighing Exercise
schedule.focus: What constitutes a material consideration, how decision-makers balance policy compliance against other factors, and why understanding this is essential to advising on development prospects.

schedule.topic: Negotiation in Planning: S106 Terms, Viability Discussions and Officer Dialogue
schedule.focus: Practical negotiation techniques used in P&D — structuring arguments with officers, building relationships pre-application, and negotiating planning obligation packages from an evidence base.

schedule.module: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism
schedule.topic: Conflicts of Interest: Identification, Disclosure and Management
schedule.focus: How to identify actual, potential and perceived conflicts of interest, RICS guidance on disclosure, independence letters, information barriers, and when to decline an instruction.
schedule.apc_competency: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism (Level 3)

schedule.topic: Anatomy of a Development Brief: Objectives, Constraints, Deliverables and Programme
schedule.focus: The essential components of a well-drafted development brief — how to capture client objectives, site and planning constraints, KPIs, team responsibilities and a realistic programme.

schedule.topic: Business Case Structure: Objectives, Options Appraisal and Financial Justification
schedule.focus: How to structure a business case for a development project — defining the strategic case, appraising options on a consistent basis, and presenting the financial case for the preferred option.

schedule.topic: Cashflow Modelling: Drawdown Schedules, S-Curves and Monitoring
schedule.focus: How to build and read a development cashflow — phasing construction spend using an S-curve, modelling revenue receipts, peak debt exposure, and using cashflow as a monitoring tool.

schedule.topic: Planning Obligations: S106 Agreements, CIL and Viability Negotiation
schedule.focus: The legal basis for S106 obligations (the three statutory tests), how CIL operates alongside S106, what viability evidence is required, and how to negotiate obligations down on a marginal scheme.

schedule.topic: CDM Regulations 2015: Duty-Holder Roles and Responsibilities
schedule.focus: Who the duty-holders are (client, principal designer, principal contractor, designer, contractor), what the pre-construction phase requires, and the surveyor's responsibilities when acting in any of these roles.

schedule.topic: Profit Metrics: Profit on Cost vs Profit on GDV, Target Returns and IRR
schedule.focus: How to calculate and interpret profit on cost, profit on GDV and internal rate of return, what benchmarks lenders and developers use, and why the choice of metric matters in appraisal sign-off.

schedule.module: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism
schedule.topic: Professional Duty to Report: Whistleblowing, Public Interest and RICS Guidance
schedule.focus: When a professional has a duty to report concerns beyond client confidentiality — RICS guidance on whistleblowing, the public interest test, protected disclosures and practical examples in P&D.
schedule.apc_competency: Ethics, Rules of Conduct and Professionalism (Level 3)

schedule.module: Conflict Avoidance, Management and Dispute Resolution Procedures
schedule.topic: ADR in Construction and Development: Mediation, Adjudication, Arbitration and Expert Determination
schedule.focus: The four main ADR methods used in construction and development disputes — how each works, when each is appropriate, relative speed and cost, and the surveyor's potential role in each.
schedule.apc_competency: Conflict Avoidance, Management and Dispute Resolution Procedures (Level 1)

schedule.topic: Stakeholder Consultation and Brief Refinement: Capturing Client and Community Needs
schedule.focus: Techniques for eliciting stakeholder requirements at the brief stage — workshops, surveys, one-to-ones — and how to reconcile conflicting priorities to produce a single agreed brief.

schedule.topic: Pre-Application Engagement: DPOs, Planning Performance Agreements and Their Strategic Value
schedule.focus: What pre-application services LPAs offer, the role of Design and Planning Officers (DPOs), when to use a Planning Performance Agreement, and how early engagement reduces application risk.

schedule.topic: Use Classes Order 2020 and Permitted Development Rights: Changes of Use and Implications
schedule.focus: The reformed Use Class E (commercial) and its implications for development flexibility, key permitted development rights for change of use, and how to advise clients on PD opportunity and risk.

schedule.topic: Finance Costs in Appraisals: Interest Calculations, Arrangement Fees and Profit Erosion
schedule.focus: How to model finance costs accurately — rolled-up vs serviced interest, arrangement and exit fees, the impact of programme overrun on finance costs, and common appraisal errors.

schedule.topic: Brief Evolution: Managing Scope Change Through RIBA Stages
schedule.focus: How and why a brief changes across RIBA stages 0–7, governance processes for authorising scope change, the cost implications of late brief changes, and the surveyor's role in brief control.

schedule.topic: Planning Research Data Sources: Portals, Census, NPPG and Local Plan Evidence Bases
schedule.focus: Key data sources used in planning and development research — Planning Portal, NPPG, ONS/census data, SHLAA/HELAA, economic and housing needs assessments — and how to handle, store and reference them.

schedule.topic: Masterplan Structure: Density, Movement Hierarchy, Land Use Mix and Public Realm
schedule.focus: The key design principles underpinning a masterplan — how density and form respond to context, structuring movement networks, achieving a balanced land use mix, and public realm as a structuring element.

schedule.topic: NPPF Presumption in Favour of Sustainable Development: Applying the Tilted Balance
schedule.focus: When the tilted balance at NPPF para 11(d) is engaged, the three 'disapply' conditions, how the tilted balance changes the weight given to the development plan, and case law on its application.

schedule.topic: Effective Project Teams in Development: Roles, Responsibilities and Inclusive Practice
schedule.focus: How high-performing development project teams are structured, the importance of clear role definition, RACI matrices, and how inclusive team practices improve decision-making and project outcomes.

schedule.topic: Comparable Evidence: Selecting, Adjusting and Applying Sales and Lettings Data
schedule.focus: How to identify appropriate comparable transactions, the adjustments required for differences in time, location, size and condition, and how to apply the comparison method to residential and commercial property.

schedule.topic: Inclusive Design Principles: The Social Model of Disability and Universal Design Hierarchy
schedule.focus: The distinction between the medical and social model of disability, the seven principles of universal design, and how these underpin inclusive environment policy and good practice at the brief and planning stages.

schedule.topic: Building Regulations Part M and BS 8300: Access Standards and the Access Audit
schedule.focus: Part M categories (M4(1), M4(2), M4(3)), when each applies, key BS 8300 requirements for the built environment, and how to structure an access audit report.

schedule.topic: RICS Property Measurement 2nd Edition: GIA, NIA, GEA and IPMS Definitions
schedule.focus: The key floor area definitions in the RICS Property Measurement professional statement, when each is used in a P&D context (appraisals, planning, agency), and common measurement errors to avoid.

schedule.topic: GIS, OS Mapping and Digital Survey Data in Development and Planning
schedule.focus: How GIS tools and Ordnance Survey data are used in site identification, constraint mapping and planning applications — including the use of Digital Terrain Models, red-line boundaries and topographic surveys.

schedule.topic: BREEAM, EPC Ratings, Net Zero Commitments and Their Role in Development Appraisals
schedule.focus: How sustainability credentials (BREEAM ratings, EPC bands, embodied carbon targets) are required by planning policy and lenders, their cost implications, and how to factor them into a development appraisal.
